---
mode: agent
description: Turn a spec/component into a detailed component design with a test plan.
---
You are running the **design** workflow (`knowledge/rigor-protocol.md`). Convene the Patterns Expert, Simplifier, and the relevant language Developer (peer), plus the AI Systems Engineer (prompt/eval/tier) and Data & Persistence Architect (schema), with the Domain Researcher establishing contracts and spikes. Define responsibility, exposed/consumed contracts (sourced), the named pattern (justified), data shapes, the error/concurrency model, a **mandatory failure-mode analysis** — derive failure modes from the design choices across the input / dependency / concurrency / state / resource / time categories, and give **each** an explicit disposition (prevent / detect / mitigate / recover / consciously accept, accepted ones with rationale + residual risk); walk **STRIDE per trust boundary** (spoofing / tampering / repudiation / disclosure / DoS / elevation) and disposition every threat (mitigate / transfer-named / accept-with-rationale) with misuse-case negative tests for mitigations — the **Security & Identity Architect holds a hard veto** on unexamined boundaries; walk **LINDDUN-lite** over every personal-data flow (or state the explicit no-personal-data line) with dispositions, retention, and rights paths — the **Privacy & Data Governance lens holds the veto**; ensure `docs/security/threat-model.md` and `docs/security/privacy-review.md` exist, link `documents` to this design, and refresh their registers via `docs-graph.py rollup`. **If the component has a user-facing interface** (UI Standard `ui-archetype-grammar.md` + `ui-archetype-catalog.md` (select the UI archetype as a determinism control) and `ui-interaction-design.md`): specify the interface — medium(s) + platform HIG, **token references / new tokens (no arbitrary values)**, key screens with hierarchy, the **complete per-component state set incl. empty/loading/error**, motion with reduced-motion, real in-voice copy, medium adaptation, **WCAG 2.2 AA + performance budget**, and for AI UIs the named **HAX** guidelines and **Shape-of-AI** patterns (Wayfinders/Tuners/Governors/Trust builders/Identifiers) with the wrong-answer and uncertainty designed as first-class — the **UX & Accessibility lens co-owns and holds the accessibility veto**; handled modes become negative/error-path tests (the cases the urge to complete skips) — the test plan **enumerating every triggered Testing Strategy directive** (a forcing function — a triggered directive left unaccounted-for fails the gate), and the **telemetry designed to the Observability & Instrumentation Standard** (`observability-and-instrumentation.md`): spans, trace-correlated structured logs, stable error codes, RFC 9457 responses for HTTP (O1–O13). Then switch to Patterns Expert + Simplifier (mutual check), Security, Distributed Systems, Test Architect, AI Systems, and Data & Persistence as adversaries — the **SRE** enforces the Instrumentation Standard and attacks the failure-mode analysis (which mode was missed?). Locate the component in the architecture's **delivery phasing** (which vertical slice it serves; which seams are mock-substitutable contracts so the slice deploys end-to-end). Write `templates/design.template.md`. Authors do not self-clear. **End with the status table:** Completed | Remaining | Best next action.


**Last action — discoverability (V10, `knowledge/knowledge-visualization.md`):** write the artifact's **frontmatter** (id, type, status, owner, typed links per the relation registry, review-by per the type's SLA, real summary — V2/V13/V14) and sync the derived index via the **script bundle** `python3 docs/ai-forward-pack/scripts/docs-graph.py derive` (`flag` for V16) — no ad-hoc scripts (V18); ensure `docs/index.html` (the Docs Explorer) exists; verify the artifact is linked into the graph; on **material change**, flag inbound neighbors `review-suggested` (V16); capture sub-ADR decisions/assumptions as linked **decision notes** in `docs/notes/` (V17) — same change as the content; frontmatter wins where the index disagrees.


**Running this in Copilot (single agent — make the dialog visible).** Copilot does not auto-spawn the personas as separate subagents the way Claude Code does, so enact the round-table *inline*: first **ground** in the prior `docs/` artifacts (spec → architecture → design) and treat them as authoritative; then for each peer named above, write a short labeled contribution in that persona's voice (e.g. **Patterns Expert —** …); then run the adversary round, each reviewer giving a labeled critique with a severity **[Blocker|Major|Minor|Nit]** and, for hard-veto roles, an explicit **PASS/BLOCK** plus the veto-clears-when predicate (`persona-cards.md` §8). Do not collapse this into one unattributed answer — the dialog is the deliverable. (Alternatively `@`-mention a specific agent from `.github/agents/` for a focused single-persona pass, or use agent **handoffs** to chain the stages.)

${input}
